req.title: Hr-Admin views training-course
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a training-course through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-048 covers an authenticated hr-admin viewing a training-course via GET /api/training-courses/{id}. The suite targets: (1) EP positive – valid hr-admin retrieves an existing course, response 200, entity id present, audit record written; (2) NEG negative – unauthenticated request yields 401; (3) NEG negative – authenticated but wrong role (employee) yields 403; (4) BVA boundary – requesting a course whose id is at the minimum valid boundary (id=1); (5) DT negative – requesting a non-existent course id yields 400 or 404 (validation/not-found). This covers all error pathways stated in the requirement and satisfies the minimum three-test floor.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_view_training_course_success
preconditions: A training course with known id exists in the system
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <get_auth_token> fixture
Seed or resolve an existing training-course id via <seed_training_course> fixture
expected: Response body contains the correct entity id
Response body includes all mandatory fields (id, title, status)
An audit record for the view action is written (verified via <audit_record_exists> helper)
trace: R-WEB-048: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_view_training_course_success(
    hr_admin_token,
    seed_training_course,
    base_url,
    audit_record_exists,
):
    course_id = seed_training_course["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/training-courses/{course_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["id"] == course_id
    for field in ("id", "title", "status"):
        assert field in body, f"Mandatory field '{field}' missing"
    assert audit_record_exists(
        action="view",
        resource="training-course",
        resource_id=course_id,
    ), "Audit record not written after successful view"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_view_training_course_returns_401
preconditions: A training course with known id exists in the system
actions: Resolve an existing training-course id via <seed_training_course> fixture
Send GET /api/training-courses/{course_id} with no Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error indicating unauthenticated access
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_view_training_course_returns_401(
    seed_training_course,
    base_url,
):
    course_id = seed_training_course["id"]

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/training-courses/{course_id}"
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, (
        "Expected error payload for 401 response"
    )

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_view_training_course_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A training course with known id exists in the system
actions: Obtain employee auth token via <get_auth_token> with role employee
Resolve an existing training-course id via <seed_training_course> fixture
Send GET /api/training-courses/{course_id} with employee Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error indicating forbidden access
trace: R-WEB-048: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_view_training_course_returns_403(
    employee_token,
    seed_training_course,
    base_url,
):
    course_id = seed_training_course["id"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"}

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/training-courses/{course_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, (
        "Expected error payload for 403 response"
    )

tests.name: test_hr_admin_view_training_course_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: A training course seeded with the minimum valid id (1) exists
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <hr_admin_token> fixture
Seed a training course explicitly at boundary id=1 via <seed_training_course_with_id> fixture
expected: All mandatory fields present in response
An audit record is written for resource_id=1
trace: R-WEB-048: boundary – minimum valid course id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_view_training_course_minimum_boundary_id(
    hr_admin_token,
    seed_training_course_with_id,
    base_url,
    audit_record_exists,
):
    BOUNDARY_ID = 1
    seed_training_course_with_id(BOUNDARY_ID)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/training-courses/{BOUNDARY_ID}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["id"] == BOUNDARY_ID
    for field in ("id", "title", "status"):
        assert field in body
    assert audit_record_exists(
        action="view",
        resource="training-course",
        resource_id=BOUNDARY_ID,
    )

tests.name: test_hr_admin_view_nonexistent_training_course_returns_400_or_404
preconditions: No training course exists for the supplied id (99999999)
actions: Obtain hr-admin auth token via <hr_admin_token> fixture
Verify the course id 99999999 does not exist via <ensure_course_absent> helper
expected: Response body contains a descriptive error message
No audit record is written for resource_id=99999999
trace: R-WEB-048: error pathway – 400 on validation error (non-existent resource treated as bad request or not-found)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_hr_admin_view_nonexistent_training_course_returns_400_or_404(
    hr_admin_token,
    base_url,
    ensure_course_absent,
    audit_record_exists,
):
    NONEXISTENT_ID = 99999999
    ensure_course_absent(NONEXISTENT_ID)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"}

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/training-courses/{NONEXISTENT_ID}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400 or 404, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    assert not audit_record_exists(
        action="view",
        resource="training-course",
        resource_id=NONEXISTENT_ID,
    ), "Audit record must NOT be written on failed view"
